 PREV CLASS   NEXT CLASS FRAMES    NO FRAMES    
SUMMARY: NESTED | FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD DETAIL: FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD

javax.media.mscontrol.networkconnection
Interface SdpPortManager

All Superinterfaces:
MediaEventNotifier<SdpPortManagerEvent>, Resource<NetworkConnection>
public interface SdpPortManager
extends Resource<NetworkConnection>, MediaEventNotifier<SdpPortManagerEvent>

Handle a set of media ports, defined by a pair of Session Descriptions.

The SdpPortManager is usually contained in a NetworkConnection.
SDP session descriptions (rfc4566) are used to setup a SdpPortManager.
A SdpPortManager can handle multiple streams (audio, video), each of them described by an SDP media description. 

 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
 -                                           stream(media description) <-rtp--->-
 -   SdpPortManager(session description)     stream(media description) <-rtp--->-
 -                                           ....                               -
 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Actually two session descriptions are needed:
  • A Media Server session description describes the mediaserver-local side of a media connection (what the media server accepts to receive)
  • A User Agent session description, describing the remote side (for example a SIPPhone)
    •  UserAgent                             Media Server
  ----                                 ---------
  -  -                                 -       -
  -  -        <-------RTP------>       -       -
  ----                                 ---------
    The SdpPortManager is compatible with the offer/answer model.

    The Relationship with SIP signaling messages is described below: 

     A) incoming INVITE with SDP offer:
  UserAgent                    JSR 309 Application         SdpPortManager
  =============================================================================
      ------INVITE----------------->
         +userAgentSDP
                                    ---------------------->processSdpOffer(userAgentSDP)
                                                                    ...................>(media server)
                                                                    <...................
                                    <-------- Event ----------------
                                        +getMediaServerSdp()
      <-------- 200 OK -------------
             +mediaServerSDP
                
      --------- ACK -------------->
           

 B) incoming INVITE without SDP:
  UserAgent                    JSR 309 Application         SdpPortManager
  =============================================================================
      ------INVITE----------------->
                                    ---------------------->generateSDPOffer()
                                                                    ...................>(media server)
                                                                    <...................
                                    <-------- Event ----------------
                                        +getMediaServerSdp()
      <-------- 200 OK -------------
             +mediaServerSDP
                
      --------- ACK -------------->
             +userAgentSDP
                                   ------------------------>processSdpAnswer(userAgentSDP)
           

 C) outgoing INVITE with SDP offer
  UserAgent                    JSR 309 Application         SdpPortManager
  =============================================================================
                                    ---------------------->generateSDPOffer()
                                                                    ...................>(media server)
                                                                    <...................
                                    <-------- Event ----------------
                                        +getMediaServerSdp()
      <------INVITE-----------------
         +mediaServerSDP

      --------- 200 OK ------------>
             +userAgentSDP
                                   ------------------------>processSdpAnswer(userAgentSDP)
                                                                    ...................>(media server)
                                                                    <...................
                                    <-------- Event ----------------
      <--------- ACK --------------
 
 d) outgoing INVITE without SDP
  UserAgent                    JSR 309 Application         SdpPortManager
  =============================================================================
      <------INVITE-----------------

      --------- 200 OK ------------>
             +userAgentSDP
                                    ---------------------->processSdpOffer(userAgentSDP)
                                                                    ...................>(media server)
                                                                    <...................
                                    <-------- Event ----------------
                                        +getMediaServerSdp()
      <--------- ACK --------------
           +mediaServerSDP
    (this is provided as a help in understanding, but the SdpPortManager has no dependency on any signaling protocol, including SIP)

    To receive the events, the Application must add a MediaEventListener, using NetworkConnection.addListener.

    Field Summary
    static Parameter SIP_HEADERS
              This parameter allows the application to pass to the SdpPortManager some SIP Headers that may be of interest for media transport, like user-agent identification.
     
    Fields inherited from interface javax.media.mscontrol.resource.Resource
    FOR_EVER, FOREVER
     
    Method Summary
     void generateSdpOffer()
              Request a SDP offer from the Media Server.
     CodecPolicy getCodecPolicy()
              Get the current CodecPolicy.
     byte[] getMediaServerSessionDescription()
              This method gives access to the Media Server session description for media streams.
     byte[] getUserAgentSessionDescription()
              This method gives access to the User Agent session description for media streams.
     void processSdpAnswer(byte[] answer)
              Request the Media Server to process the given SDP answer (from the remote User Agent).
     void processSdpOffer(byte[] offer)
              Request the MediaServer to process the given SDP offer (from the remote User Agent).
     void rejectSdpOffer()
              Cancel the SDP offer that previously was requested with generateSdpOffer().
     void setCodecPolicy(CodecPolicy policy)
              Install the given CodecPolicy.
     
    Methods inherited from interface javax.media.mscontrol.resource.Resource
    getContainer
     
    Methods inherited from interface javax.media.mscontrol.MediaEventNotifier
    addListener, getMediaSession, removeListener
     

    Field Detail

    SIP_HEADERS

    static final Parameter SIP_HEADERS
    This parameter allows the application to pass to the SdpPortManager some SIP Headers that may be of interest for media transport, like user-agent identification.
    The expected value is a Map<String, String>, where each entry is a (header-name, header-value).

    Method Detail

    generateSdpOffer

    void generateSdpOffer()
                      throws SdpPortManagerException
    Request a SDP offer from the Media Server.
    When complete, sends a SdpPortManagerEvent with an EventType of SdpPortManagerEvent.OFFER_GENERATED.
    The resulting offer is available with SdpPortManagerEvent.getMediaServerSdp()

    This can be used to initiate a connection, or to increase/augment the capabilities of an established connection, like for example adding a video stream to an audio-only connection.

    Throws:
    SdpPortManagerException

    processSdpOffer

    void processSdpOffer(byte[] offer)
                     throws SdpPortManagerException
    Request the MediaServer to process the given SDP offer (from the remote User Agent).
    When complete, sends a SdpPortManagerEvent with an EventType of SdpPortManagerEvent.ANSWER_GENERATED.
    The resulting answer is available with SdpPortManagerEvent.getMediaServerSdp()

    Parameters:
    offer - SDP offer from the remote User Agent
    Throws:
    SdpPortManagerException

    processSdpAnswer

    void processSdpAnswer(byte[] answer)
                      throws SdpPortManagerException
    Request the Media Server to process the given SDP answer (from the remote User Agent).
    When complete, sends a SdpPortManagerEvent with an EventType of SdpPortManagerEvent.ANSWER_PROCESSED;

    Parameters:
    answer - SDP answer from the remote User Agent
    Throws:
    SdpPortManagerException

    rejectSdpOffer

    void rejectSdpOffer()
                    throws SdpPortManagerException
    Cancel the SDP offer that previously was requested with generateSdpOffer().
    The media server is allowed to free the resources associated to the offer (including RTP ports).
    Usages of this method include a SDP "glare" case, see RFC 3261.

    Note that in usual operation, the media server resources are released when the containing NetworkConnection is released, and the application is not required to call this method.

    Throws:
    SdpPortManagerException

    getMediaServerSessionDescription

    byte[] getMediaServerSessionDescription()
                                        throws SdpPortManagerException
    This method gives access to the Media Server session description for media streams.
    This session description describes what the media server is willing to accept, according to the offer/answer algorithm.

    Note : This method returns the media previously agreed after a complete offer-answer exchange. If no media has been agreed yet, it returns null. If an offer is in progress from either side, that offer's session description is not returned here.
    As a result, the offer generated by     generateSdpOffer() must be retrieved from the completion event, see SdpPortManagerEvent.getMediaServerSdp().

    Returns:
    The last agreed Media Server session description
    Throws:
    SdpPortManagerException - if no session description is available (for example, before any call)

    getUserAgentSessionDescription

    byte[] getUserAgentSessionDescription()
                                      throws SdpPortManagerException
    This method gives access to the User Agent session description for media streams.
    The User Agent is the "remote" end (for example a SipPhone), by opposition to the Media Server end.

    Note : This method returns the media previously agreed after a complete offer-answer exchange. If no media has been agreed yet, it returns null.

    Returns:
    The last agreed User Agent session description
    Throws:
    SdpPortManagerException - if the UserAgent Session Description has not been set yet.

    setCodecPolicy

    void setCodecPolicy(CodecPolicy policy)
                    throws SdpPortManagerException
    Install the given CodecPolicy.
    It will be enforced when the connection is setup.

    If the policy enforcement causes a setup failure, the SdpPortManagerEvent will hold the error SdpPortManagerEvent.SDP_NOT_ACCEPTABLE.

    Throws:
    SdpPortManagerException - if the CodecPolicy is incoherent (for example, a codec is both excluded and required)

    getCodecPolicy

    CodecPolicy getCodecPolicy()
    Get the current CodecPolicy.
    The default CodecPolicy has all the codecs and media types allowed.
